questions.text: Where are you based?
What is your gender?
How tall are you?
What would a friend say about your build?
How open are you to relocating for a relationship?
What are your drinking habits?
options: Socially — a few times a month
Regularly — a few times a week

questions.text: When you drink, what does that typically look like for you?
options: A glass of wine or beer at home
Drinking heavily when I do drink

questions.text: Do you smoke cigarettes?
Do you use marijuana?
How often do you exercise?
What is your typical sleep schedule?
options: Early bird — up before 7am, in bed by 10pm
Standard — up by 8am, in bed around midnight
Night owl — up past midnight, sleep in late

questions.text: What does your ideal weekend look like?
options: Active outdoors — hiking, sports, being outside
Going out — bars, restaurants, events, nightlife
Relaxed socializing — dinners at home, small gatherings
Fully recharging alone — couch, movies, nothing planned
Working on something — side projects, creative work, hustle
Traveling or getting out of town
It genuinely depends on my mood

questions.text: Do you have pets?
What are your hobbies and interests?
What is your cleanliness level at home?
options: Very tidy — everything has a place and I keep it that way
I'll be honest, it gets pretty messy

questions.text: Do you use recreational drugs beyond cannabis?
options: No — I don't use recreational drugs
Occasionally — a few times a year at most
Sometimes — a few times a month

questions.text: When it comes to hobbies and daily life, what are you looking for in a partner?
options: A true adventure partner — I want someone who actively does life with me
Mostly shared — I want real overlap, but we can have our own things too
A healthy mix — some shared interests, plenty of independence
Independent is great — I love having my own world and theirs

questions.text: How would you describe your ambition level?
options: Extremely driven — work is central to my identity
Moderately ambitious — I work to live

questions.text: On a typical weekday evening, what does your time usually look like?
options: Working late — evenings are often part of my day
Working on personal projects or side work

questions.text: How financially stable do you currently feel?
options: Very stable — I feel secure and have savings
Stable — I'm comfortable but not building much
Getting there — I'm working on it
Struggling — finances are a real stressor right now

questions.text: What is your highest level of education?
How would you describe your financial style?
options: Saver — I prioritize building security and rarely splurge
Balanced — I save and spend intentionally
Spender — I enjoy my money and live in the moment
Working on it — I want to be better with money

questions.text: Long-term, where do you picture yourself living?
options: A city — culture, career energy, and proximity to everything
Suburbs — space and community, close to the city when I want it
Rural or a small town — quiet, land, and a slower pace
Wherever makes sense — the right person matters more than the place

questions.text: How close are you with your family?
options: Very close — family is central to my life
Close — we're connected but I have my own independence
Somewhat close — we have a relationship but it's complicated
Not close — I've built my own chosen family

questions.text: How important is family in your life?
options: Very important — a major part of how I live
Important but balanced with my independence
Not a primary focus for me right now

questions.text: How much time alone do you need to feel recharged?
options: A lot — I need significant solo time to feel like myself
Some — a few hours or a day here and there
A little — I mostly recharge through people and connection
Very little — being around others energizes me

questions.text: How would you describe your social life?
options: Very social — I have a big circle and love being out
Social but selective — I have close friends and meaningful events
Quieter — I have a small circle and prefer depth over breadth
Mostly solo — I'm fairly independent and introverted

questions.text: How would you describe your political views?
options: Apolitical — politics isn't a big part of my life

questions.text: How would you describe your religious or spiritual beliefs?
options: Actively religious — faith is a major part of my life
Culturally religious — I identify with a tradition but don't actively practice
Atheist — I don't believe in a god or higher power
I prefer not to label it

questions.text: How actively do you practice your faith or spirituality?
options: Very actively — it's woven into my daily life
Regularly — weekly services, rituals, or practice
Occasionally — holidays, personal moments, or when I need it
Rarely — it's more of a background identity than a practice

questions.text: When someone you're dating takes longer than usual to respond, you usually:
options: Assume they're busy and don't stress about it
Feel uneasy and find myself checking my phone
Feel irritated and start to pull back
Tell myself I don't care, but I'm still thinking about it

questions.text: When a relationship starts getting emotionally close:
options: It feels natural — I lean into it
It feels exciting but also makes me nervous
It starts feeling like too much and I need space
I want it, then feel the urge to pull away

questions.text: When something is bothering you in a relationship, how do you communicate it?
options: I say it directly as soon as I feel it
I wait until I've had time to process, then bring it up
I bring it up, but I worry about how they'll react
I tend to drop hints and hope they notice
I write or message first, then talk in person
I struggle to bring it up and often let it go

questions.text: How much space does your most significant past relationship still take up in your life or thinking?
options: None — that chapter feels genuinely closed
Some — it comes to mind occasionally but doesn't affect me
A fair amount — I'm still working through parts of it
A lot — I'm honestly not fully over it yet

questions.text: When you're going through a stressful period at work or in life, what does that tend to look like in your relationships?
options: I lean on my partner more — I need closeness when life feels heavy
I pull back and need space to process before I can open up
I try to keep it separate — I don't want to burden the people I love
I lose patience more easily and I know it affects how I show up

questions.text: After a fight or disagreement with someone you care about, you typically:
options: Want to reconnect quickly — the tension feels worse to me than the fight itself
Give it a day, then come back ready to move on
Need the other person to acknowledge what happened before I can move forward
Hold onto it for a while — I genuinely find it hard to let things go

questions.text: When your partner shares something small — a funny thing they saw, a worry, something they're excited about — what's your natural instinct?
options: Stop what I'm doing and fully engage — those moments matter to me
Respond warmly but keep it light — I'm present but don't make it a big moment
Acknowledge it but I'm often still focused on what I was doing
I tend to stay in my own headspace — I'm not naturally great at this

questions.text: How do you feel when a partner maintains a close friendship with someone they've previously been attracted to?
options: Comfortable — trust matters more than history
Fine, but I'd appreciate some transparency about the friendship
It depends on the nature of the friendship and how it's handled
I'd find it difficult — I prefer clearer boundaries around this

questions.text: How do you feel about therapy or couples counseling as part of a healthy relationship?
options: I'm a strong believer — I think everyone benefits from it
I'm open to it if things get hard
I'd consider it, but it's not something I'd seek proactively
I'm skeptical — I prefer handling things privately

questions.text: When your partner does something that bothers you repeatedly, your instinct is usually to:
options: Bring it up directly, staying curious about where they're coming from
Let the small things go and only raise the bigger patterns
Say something, but I know I can be harsher than I intend
Go quiet and pull away rather than engage
Bring it up, but I sometimes catastrophize or make it personal

questions.text: Do you currently have children?
options: Yes — children who live with their other parent

questions.text: Do you want children in the future?
options: Open to it if my partner wants them
I have young children and would like more
I have young children and I'm done
My children are grown — I'm not looking to have more
My children are grown — open to a partner with young children
I'm open to being a stepparent
I prefer a partner without young children at home

questions.text: What are your views on marriage?
options: Open to marriage if it feels right

questions.text: What relationship pace feels natural to you?
options: Fast if the connection is strong

questions.text: What are you looking for right now?
options: A relationship that could become serious
I'm open, but ultimately looking for something meaningful

questions.text: When it comes to raising children, how would you describe your approach?
options: Structure and clear expectations — consistency is how children feel secure
Emotional attunement above all — I want my kids to feel deeply understood
A balance — firm on some things, relaxed and flexible on others
Autonomy and experience — I want to raise independent, curious thinkers

questions.text: Which genders are you interested in being matched with?
Which sexual orientations are you open to in a partner?
What age range are you open to dating?
What height range are you open to dating?
How far away are you open to dating someone right now?
options: Anywhere within my state or region
Open to anywhere in the world

questions.text: What body types are you typically attracted to?
What drinking habits are you comfortable with in a partner?
When it comes to drinking, what are you looking for in a partner?
options: I want someone who drinks with me — it's part of how I connect and socialize
I don't need them to drink — I'm happy either way

questions.text: What smoking habits are you comfortable with in a partner?
Are you okay with a partner who uses marijuana?
options: No — I prefer they don't use at all

questions.text: How active of a lifestyle are you looking for in a partner?
How would you feel about a partner who has pets?
options: I love pets — any pet is fine
Dogs are fine but I'm not a cat person
Cats are fine but I'm not a dog person
Small pets only (no dogs or cats)
I cannot be around pets due to allergies

questions.text: How do you feel about a partner who uses recreational drugs beyond cannabis?
options: Not okay with it — this is important to me
Prefer they don't, but occasional or past use is fine

questions.text: When it comes to recreational drugs, what are you looking for in a partner?
options: I want someone who participates with me — it's part of how I experience life
I don't mind either way — they don't need to join me

questions.text: In a serious relationship, how do you expect household responsibilities to be divided?
options: Whoever has more bandwidth at the time
I'd carry more — I have high standards and I'm okay with that
I'd expect my partner to carry more — I contribute in other ways
Whoever cares more about it handles it — I'm easy-going about this

questions.text: What political views are you most compatible with in a partner?
What religious or spiritual beliefs are you most compatible with in a partner?
How important is it that your partner participates in your faith or spiritual practice?
options: Very important — I want someone who practices alongside me
Somewhat important — I want them to respect and engage with it, even if they don't practice
Not important — I'm fine if they have a different or no practice

questions.text: How important is it that your partner has similar views on marriage?
How important is similar ambition level in a partner?
How important is it that your partner shares your approach to parenting?
options: Very important — this is a genuine priority for me
Moderately important — I'd want to understand their approach
Somewhat important — I think we could navigate differences
Not very important — I trust we'd figure it out together

questions.text: How important is physical intimacy in a relationship to you?
options: Central — it's one of the primary ways I feel connected and loved
Important — it matters significantly but isn't the whole picture
Moderately important — I connect more through emotional intimacy
Less important — emotional closeness is what I prioritize

questions.text: How important is it that your partner has a similar level of physical intimacy needs?
options: Very important — strong alignment here matters to me
Moderately important — some alignment would be good
Somewhat important — I think we'd figure it out
Not a major factor for me
